pi_items.source: Code analysis - setup_task.sh downloads BMW27.blend.zip from https://download.blender.org/demo/test/BMW27.blend.zip and extracts to this path
Code analysis - setup_task.sh creates corrupted mesh and saves to this path using Blender Python script
Code analysis - setup_task.sh removes previous output, task description states this is the save destination, export_result.sh checks for this file

Code analysis - verifier.py (lines 80-88) awards 25/25 points only when duplicates == 0, not when duplicates <= 5. Verifier gives 15/25 points if duplicates < 10. Metadata value does not match verifier's passing threshold.
Code analysis - verifier.py (lines 91-96) awards 20/20 points only when loose_verts == 0. Metadata matches verifier threshold.

Code analysis - verifier.py (lines 99-107) awards 20/20 points only when degenerate_faces == 0, not when <= 2. Verifier gives 10/20 points if degenerate_faces < 5. Metadata value does not match verifier's passing threshold.
Code analysis - verifier.py (lines 116-123) awards 25/25 points when bad_ratio < 0.01 (which is 1%), not 2%. Metadata value is 2.0% but verifier uses 1% as the passing threshold.
Code analysis - setup_task.sh (line 105) duplicates min(150, len(verts)) vertices in place
Code analysis - setup_task.sh (lines 109-113) creates 80 loose vertices at random positions
Code analysis - setup_task.sh (lines 118-120) flips normals on 30% of faces randomly
Code analysis - setup_task.sh (lines 124-131) creates ~20 degenerate faces with near-zero area
Web search and file fetch verification - confirmed BMW27.blend.zip exists on official Blender download server. Created by Mike Pan as a Blender benchmark.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official Blender BMW27.blend demo file (a real asset from the Blender project). The corrupted mesh is created programmatically with: ~150 duplicate vertex pairs, 80 loose vertices, ~30% flipped normals, and ~20 degenerate faces. The metadata contains three contradicted threshold values: max_duplicates (metadata claims 5, verifier requires 0), max_degenerate (metadata claims 2, verifier requires 0), and max_inconsistent_normals_percent (metadata claims 2.0%, verifier requires <1.0%).
